Because fast racing begins with fast training. Available now at link in bio.

node.text:
Behind every great athlete is a coach who helps them reach their potential. 

Meet Patrick Sang, coach to Eliud Kipchoge and Geoffrey Kamworor, who believes that helping athletes become better human beings is just as important as the physical work of training. Since he started coaching in 1995, Patrick has seen many of his athletes win titles and break world records. He also stood at the finish line as Eliud broke the two-hour marathon barrier last October, and since that day, the two have been preparing for Eliud to break his World Record in London.

Watch the film to learn more about the guiding philosophies of this legendary coach.

node.title: More than a Coach: Patrick Sang

node.text:
Go behind the design and learn about the making of our workhorse with wings. Hear from the visionaries, athletes, and coaches who count on the Pegasus, year after year.
⠀
Featuring a new Air Zoom unit beneath the forefoot and Nike React foam in the midsole, the latest Pegasus offers lightweight reliability for your daily runs. Available now in select areas.

node.title: Behind the Design - Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 37

node.text:
For two weeks, Blue Ribbon Sports and BRS Manager, @jjeffersoniv. traveled across the US to meet and learn from runners coast to coast. With sound advice from Nike’s First Employee, Jeff Johnson, we met runners face-to-face in their communities of Los Angeles, Chicago, and New York City. We learned from them, celebrated running, and sold exclusive BRS footwear along the way.
⠀
Throughout the tour, we met amazing runners with inspiring stories about why they started running, the importance of their respective community and the power of the run. Since our early beginnings, we’ve always known that the best way to serve the runner is face-to-face, and with Blue Ribbon Sports we’ve set out to do just that.
⠀
Watch the film and let us know where in the world you want to see BRS pop-up next.  #blueribbonsports @vw

node.text:
12 runners, 10 countries, 5 languages, 1 epic adventure.
⠀
Last week, 12 runners out of the hundreds of thousands who completed the Go More, Get More challenge on the Nike Run Club app assembled at Nike headquarters and ran Hood To Coast, a 199-mile relay race from Oregon’s Mt. Hood to its breathtaking coastline.
⠀
Before the race, these 12 people were strangers. On the course, they were teammates. And by the finish line, they became lifelong friends. Watch the film and see how running brought them together.

node.text:
NIKE MIND INTAKE SESSION

Athlete:
Claire Prince
Runner / Boxer / Nike Trainer

Key finding:
“It’s all muscle memory. I just need to execute.”

Brought to you by Nike Mind: A MIND-ALTERING SHOE. Restocks coming soon.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo shared by Nike Running on January 08, 2026 tagging @iamthebatman___. May be an image of one or more people, braids, people playing tennis, sportswear, racket, sports equipment and text.

node.text:
A MIND-ALTERING SHOE.

Nike Mind activates your senses to enhance your pregame routine. 

For runner, boxer, and Nike Trainer @iamthebatman___ [Claire Prince], that means keeping her cool even when the odds are against her.

Available now.

node.text:
For over a century, Japanese students have run in a relay race that puts both the heart and the spirit to the test.

And only one team’s tasuki can cross the line first.

日本の学生達が100年以上つないで来たレース。
努力、想い、そして魂を一本の襷に込めて仲間たちに託される。

手渡しされる襷は、目に見える。
でもそれは、見えないすべてからできているできている。
積み重ねた力をつなげ。

If it was easy, it wouldn’t be NXN.

Welcome to Nike Cross Nationals—the hardest 16 minutes of the year. The carnage begins tomorrow.

node.text:
8 amateur runners. 1 podium dream. 

Nike Project Speed: Road To Podium.

Tap the link in bio to watch the full documentary, out now on YouTube.

5,000 women lit up London for an After Dark Tour like no other.

1K to find their opponents. 10K to race them. When the spotlight shone, the city showed up.

@kipchogeeliud proves that no human is limited. Not by doubt. Not by distance. And never by time. 

Greatness doesn’t ask if there’s a finish line. It asks: where do we run next?
